characters.name: Aaliyah
characters.description: AaliyahBiographical InformationStatusAliveFamilial InformationChildrenExpectingAppearancesOnlyGA:Bottle Up and Explode!S19:Things We Lost in the FireSeasonsGA:18S19:5Portrayed byKimberly Aria Peterson Aaliyahis a pregnant woman who was injured in a gas main explosion. Aaliyah was nearby when a gas main exploded. She was taken to triage, where she was given a yellow tag. But then her blood pressure was elevated and she began vomiting, so Travis replaced her yellow tag with a red one and texted Carina to come to the site on foot to check on her. When Carina arrived, she took over Aaliyah's treatment.("Things We Lost in the Fire")

characters.name: Aaron
characters.description: AaronBiographical InformationStatusAliveMedical InformationDiagnosisPTSDDoctor(s)Sheldon WallaceTreatmentTalk therapyFamilial InformationMaritalSissy(wife)Professional InformationProfessionPolice OfficerWorkplaceLos Angeles Metro PoliceAppearancesOnlyThe Standing Eight CountSeasonsPP:5Portrayed byEdwin Hodge Aaronis a police office who had PTSD after fatally shooting someone on the job. Aaron was in a session with Sheldon. He talked about the incident that led him to therapy. He and his partner had been following a car after an armed robbery. The driver ran out of the car, so Aaron followed him. He cornered him. The man reached for his waistband, so Aaron shot him. He later found out the man was unarmed. He said he felt like he'd followed protocol. Sheldon, believing there was more to it, asked if he was sleeping. Aaron said he just wanted to get back to work. When Sheldon pressed, he said that he sometimes sees things on the job that he can't describe to his wife. Sheldon said Aaron could talk to him about it. Their session was interrupted byJoe Price, who said he needed to take Aaron home. Sheldon said Aaron wasn't ready to go back to work. Joe said he needed Sheldon to sign off on him returning to work because internal affairs was still investigating and Sheldon not signing off would be a red flag.

characters.name: Aaron
characters.description: AaronBiographical InformationStatusAliveAppearancesOnlyLet's Talk About SexSeasonsGA:19Portrayed byRhyan Hill Aaronis one of the teens who attended the sexual education presentation atGrey Sloan Memorial Hospital. Aaron was attending a sexual education presentation the doctors at Grey Sloan Memorial were putting on in the hopes of educating teens and reducing incidents of teen pregnancies. During the presentation, Aaron asked why what they said didn't reflect what he'd seen in pornography, they said that pornography was very different from reality and if you have sex like you've seen in pornography, it's likely your partner is feeling little to no pleasure. Aaron disagreed, but they said that was likely because his partner was putting on a show.

characters.name: Aaron

characters.name: Aaron
characters.description: AaronBiographical InformationStatusAliveMedical InformationDiagnosisScalp lacerationDoctor(s)Simone GriffithWes BryantTreatmentSkin glueAppearancesOnlyWhen I CrashSeasonsGA:22Portrayed byJ Chase Bailey Aaronis a patient who was in a car accident. Aaron was in a car with his aide, Glen, when they were in a car accident. Aaron rode in the ambulance with Glen to the hospital, but ran from the ambulance when they arrived. He was agitated, butWes Bryantwas able to connect with him after finding Aaron's basketball in his bag. He was then able to get Aaron to agree to come into the ER and get onto a gurney. However, when Wes tried to examine him, he pushed Wes's hands away. Simone had the idea to put a visor on Aaron so he couldn't see Wes's hands approaching, allowing him to play his game as Wes worked. He was able to clean Aaron's wound and glue it closed.

characters.name: Aarón
characters.description: AarónBiographical InformationStatusAliveFamilial InformationSiblingsJeniParentsFatherMother †AppearancesOnlyStarted from the BottomSeasonsS19:5Portrayed byMichael Mourra Aarónis a child who came to the station with his sister to seek help. Aarón was home with his sister, Jeni, when Jeni began menstruating for the first time. Unaware of what was happening, Jeni tried to use bandages to stop the bleeding, but when that didn't work, she and Aarón became worried that she was going to die, as their mother had bled out and died at a hospital. Refusing to go to a hospital, they walked to station 19 to get help. As Aarón and Jenny started to talk about what was happening, Carina and Vic quickly realized she'd just gotten her first period. Carina assured Aarón that Jeni wasn't going to die and asked him for twenty minutes to talk to her before they went home, even giving him her phone so he could time it. While Carina and Vic were talking to Jeni, Jack took Aarón into the barn to clean up the cuts he got when he fell on the way to the station. Aarón was still concerned about what was happening to his sister, so Jack explained menstruation to him in very simple terms. They then went back into the station, where they tossed gummy bears into a mug until Jeni came downstairs ready to go home. Aarón gave her the leftover gummy bears and Jack walked the two of them back home.

characters.name: Aasif
characters.description: AasifBiographical InformationStatusAliveAppearancesOnlySomething's Gotta GiveSeasonsGA:7Portrayed byOmid AbtahiAasifworks for the Emir. Aasif came to the hospital with the Emir after he was injured in a car accident. He was extremely worried and provided the doctors with his medical history. When the Emir needed surgery, Assif confessed that he'd been diagnosed with an unruptured aneurysm. So they called inDerek Shepherdto operate on his head while they operated on his other injuries. He was worried because he'd promised not to tell anyone about the aneurysm until after the election. However, Meredith assured him that he was only trying to save the Emir's life. He works for the Emir. He says that the Emir is like a father to him.

characters.name: Charlie Abbott
characters.description: Charlie AbbottBiographical InformationStatusAliveMedical InformationDiagnosisHead injuryDoctor(s)Derek ShepherdTobyTreatmentStaplesFamilial InformationParentsFatherAppearancesOnlyHow to Save a LifeSeasonsGA:11Portrayed byRobby RasmussenCharlie Abbottwas helped byDerek Shepherdafter being in a car accident. The morning of the crash, Charlie asked a classmate,Alana, to go for a drive with him. She accepted and he took her in his dad's car on a mostly deserted road. While they were driving, Charlie was consistently speeding, dangerously weaving as he passed other cars. He successfully passedDerek Shepherd, but when he went to passSarah'scar, he clipped the front end, sending both cars flying. Alana and Charlie were both ejected from the vehicle, likely because they weren't wearing seat belts.

characters.name: Abby
characters.description: AbbyBiographical InformationStatusAliveFamilial InformationSiblingsJonnyParentsBrendaAppearancesOnlyAlone in the DarkSeasonsS19:5Portrayed bySydney Mae Estrella Abbyis a child who got separated from her mother during a parking garage fire. Abby and her brother,Jonny, were out with their mother when she stopped at a grocery store to get chocolate milk. She left Abby and Jonny in the car while she went inside. While she was gone, a fire started in the parking garage. When Jonny was rendered unconscious, Abby struggled to wake him so they could get away from the fire. She was trying to pull him out whenBenandVicfound them and got them out to safety. Jonny was loaded onto a gurney and he and Abby were taken to the hospital.

characters.name: Abena
characters.description: AbenaBiographical InformationStatusAliveMedical InformationDiagnosisSickle-cell anemiaDoctor(s)Miranda BaileyAlex KarevTreatmentHydroxyureaFamilial InformationSiblingsLeboOthersNephewAppearancesOnlyI Will SurviveSeasonsGA:7Portrayed byAnehita OkojieAbenais one of the children who came to Seattle for surgery through the African program. Abena had come to Seattle to have her surgery. After a month, she was finally ready to go home. They reminded her to take her hydroxyurea to treat her sickle-cell anemia, but as they were leaving, her sister,Lebo, collapsed outside the hospital, so she had to stay longer. Her sister, Lebo, came to the hospital with her.

characters.name: Kamal Aboud
characters.description: Kamal AboudBiographical InformationStatusAliveMedical InformationDiagnosisOllier's diseaseEnchondromasDoctor(s)Callie TorresJackson AveryBen WarrenTreatmentTumor resectionsHand reconstructionAppearancesOnlyThe Me Nobody KnowsSeasonsGA:12Portrayed byElisha Henig Kamal Aboudis a boy from Jordan who had serious tumors invading his hands. Kamal was living in Jordan. He had to watch his entire family being killed and would have been taken as a child soldier, except that his hands were deformed. His home was burned down, so he lived in the hospital in Jordan, where the doctors gave him odd jobs.

characters.name: Adam
characters.description: AdamBiographical InformationStatusAliveAppearancesOnlyIdle HandsSeasonsGA:9Portrayed byHerbie JacksonAdamis one of the students ofMadeleine Skurskiwho visited her in the hospital. He is in Ms. Skurski's math and science class. When Ms. Skurski became ill,Emma,Lucas, and Adam skipped their after-school study session to see her. They then returned with the rest of their class, which is when Ms. Skurski broke the news that she wouldn't be returning to school. He loves his teacher, Ms. Skurski. When Ms. Skurski got sick and Mr. Arnold replaced her, he came to the hospital to see Ms. Skurski.

characters.name: Adam
characters.description: AdamBiographical InformationStatusAliveProfessional InformationProfessionPersonal assistantAppearancesOnlyIn Which Dell Finds His FightSeasonsPP:1Portrayed byJocko Sims AdamisJack Grossman'spersonal assistant. Adam was taken Cooper and Pete's parenting class. He was enthusiastic and offered to try things first. At the end of the first session, he confessed to them that he wasn't actually an expectant father. He was attending the class for his boss, who was expecting a baby. They told him that his boss needed to attend the class for himself.

characters.name: Bill Adams
characters.description: Bill AdamsBiographical InformationStatusAliveMedical InformationDiagnosisGonadal hermaphroditismBlind vas deferensDoctor(s)Preston BurkeDr. KnoxAlex KarevGeorge O'MalleyTreatmentOophorectomyFamilial InformationMaritalHolly Adams(wife)AppearancesOnlyWho's Zoomin' Who?SeasonsGA:1Portrayed byWayne Wilderson Bill Adamswas a patient atSeattle Grace Hospitaland friend ofPreston Burke. Bill came to Seattle Grace Hospital abdominal pain and blood in his urine. After an endoscopy, Burke ordered a biopsy and discovered that Bill had an ovary. Bill went into surgery to have it removed, but another doctor noted that the Holly's baby couldn't possibly be his because he had blind vas deferens. Burke confronted Holly and she said if Burke was a good friend then he wouldn't tell Bill that she had cheated on him but Burke told him he was sterile anyway, owing it to him as a doctor.

characters.name: Doug Adams
characters.description: Doug AdamsBiographical InformationStatusAliveAliasMr. Midlife CrisisMedical InformationDoctor(s)Violet TurnerTreatmentTalk therapyFamilial InformationMaritalKaren Adams†(wife)AppearancesFirstIn Which Addison Finds the MagicLastCrime and PunishmentCount2SeasonsPP:1and2Portrayed byAndy Milder Doug Adamsis a patient who spent three years in therapy getting up the courage to leave his wife. After 36 months of seeing Violet, Doug felt that he was prepared to ask his wife for a divorce, something he'd wanted for a while. After a successful session, he went home and asked her for one, but she became angry at him and dragged him back down to the practice, where she confronted Violet. During the confrontation, she had a serious nosebleed, so Sam took her into a room and tried to cauterize the bleed while Doug went to talk to Violet again. Doug was resolute in his decision, but when he went to tell Karen he was leaving, he found that the bleeding had worsened. When Karen was then diagnosed with Wegener's disease, he decided to stay with her, even after learning that she had known about it for six months and hadn't told him.("In Which Addison Finds the Magic")

characters.name: Ian Adams
characters.description: Ian AdamsBiographical InformationStatusAliveAppearancesOnlyWhistlin' Past the GraveyardSeasonsGA:16Portrayed byKailen Jude Ian Adamsis a peds patient who struggled to find a Halloween costume. Ian found Levi and asked for his help picking a Halloween costume. Levi went through the options, but Ian didn't want any of them. When Levi asked him what he wanted to be, he said he wanted to be a sunflower. Levi made two sunflower costumes, one for himself and one for Ian, which he wore to the costume parade.

characters.name: Jerry Adams
characters.description: Jerry AdamsBiographical InformationStatusAliveMedical InformationDiagnosisHuman papilomavirusImmune deficiencyDoctor(s)Miranda BaileyMark SloanLexie GreyJackson AveryTreatmentWart removalSkin graftsFamilial InformationMaritalTess Adams(wife, separated)SiblingsBrothersAppearancesOnlySuperfreakSeasonsGA:7Portrayed byArt Chudabala Jerry Adamsis a man whose HPV had grown out of control, covering his body in warts. Jerry's wife, Tess, brought him to the ER. He refused to get out of the car because he was embarrassed by his appearance. He had warts covering most of his body.Mark Sloantold him he'd contracted HPV at some point and an immune deficiency allowed the warts to grow out of control. He'd seen a dermatologist before and had warts removed, but they grew back, even after a second trip. He was told he'd need surgery to remove them, but he just wanted to give up. However, his wife insisted that he have the surgery or she would leave him.

characters.name: Karen Adams
characters.description: Karen AdamsBiographical InformationStatusDeceasedMedical InformationDiagnosisNosebleedWegener's diseaseDoctor(s)Sam BennettTreatmentCauterizationFamilial InformationMaritalDoug Adams(husband)AppearancesOnlyIn Which Addison Finds the MagicSeasonsPP:1Portrayed byMolly HaganKaren Adamsis a woman who had Wegener's disease who was ultimately killed by her husband in what he claimed was a mercy killing. Karen came into the practice enraged after her husband told her he wanted a divorce. She blamed Violet for planting the idea in his mind. During the confrontation, Karen had a nosebleed, so Sam took her into a room to cauterize it. Doug later came back after speaking with Violet resolute in his decision to ask for a divorce, but hesitated when he saw that the bleeding had worsened. Sam believed that she had Wegener's disease, which would ultimately be fatal. He went to run tests to confirm, but learned that Karen had already been diagnosed atSt. Ambrose Hospitalsix months prior. Despite this, Doug decided to stay with Karen, saying it's what he wanted.("In Which Addison Finds the Magic") Karen was later shot to death by her husband. He initially claimed that it was a mercy killing and she asked him to do it, but later confessed that he had shot her in cold blood.("Crime and Punishment")

characters.name: Karen Adams
characters.description: Karen AdamsBiographical InformationStatusDeceasedMedical InformationDiagnosisNosebleedWegener's diseaseDoctor(s)Sam BennettTreatmentCauterizationFamilial InformationMaritalDoug Adams(husband)AppearancesOnlyIn Which Addison Finds the MagicSeasonsPP:1Portrayed byMolly HaganKaren Adamsis a woman who had Wegener's disease who was ultimately killed by her husband in what he claimed was a mercy killing. Karen came into the practice enraged after her husband told her he wanted a divorce. She blamed Violet for planting the idea in his mind. During the confrontation, Karen had a nosebleed, so Sam took her into a room to cauterize it. Doug later came back after speaking with Violet resolute in his decision to ask for a divorce, but hesitated when he saw that the bleeding had worsened. Sam believed that she had Wegener's disease, which would ultimately be fatal. He went to run tests to confirm, but learned that Karen had already been diagnosed atSt. Ambrose Hospitalsix months prior. Despite this, Doug decided to stay with Karen, saying it's what he wanted.("In Which Addison Finds the Magic") Karen was later shot to death by her husband. He initially claimed that it was a mercy killing and she asked him to do it, but later confessed that he had shot her in cold blood.("Crime and Punishment")

characters.name: Lucas Adams
characters.description: Lucas Shepherd AdamsBiographical InformationStatusAliveAliasShep[1]Skywalker[2]LukeTitle(s)M.D.Familial InformationSiblingsFour sisters[3]GrandparentsCarolyn ShepherdMr. Shepherd†OthersDerek Shepherd†(uncle)Amelia Shepherd(aunt)Kathleen ShepherdLiz ShepherdNancy ShepherdMeredith Grey(aunt)Christopher Shepherd†(cousin)Scout Derek Shepherd Lincoln(cousin)Zola Grey Shepherd(cousin)Derek Bailey Shepherd(cousin)Ellis Shepherd(cousin)Professional InformationProfessionSurgicalResidentWorkplaceGrey Sloan Memorial HospitalAppearancesFirstGA:Everything Has ChangedOnlyS19:Everybody's Got Something to Hide Except Me and My MonkeyCount65SeasonsGA:19,20,21, and22S19:6Portrayed byNiko Terho Lucas Adamsis a second-yearsurgicalresidentatGrey Sloan Memorial Hospital. He was in a relationship withSimone Griffith. Lucas grew up in Connecticut as part of the Shepherd family.[4]Despite pressure from his family, he didn't want to become a doctor until he was in a bad car accident his junior year of college, which gave him a new appreciation for the doctors who saved his life.[5] He is the charming black sheep of his family. Likeable to a fault, he has a great mind, but doesn't have the grades to match. He's determined to prove himself as a surgeon, just like many in his family that have come before him, but he will have to stop relying on his people skills and put in the work.

He's determined to prove himself as a surgeon, just like many in his family that have come before him, but he will have to stop relying on his people skills and put in the work.

characters.name: Tess Adams
characters.description: Tess AdamsBiographical InformationStatusAliveFamilial InformationMaritalJerry Adams(husband, seperated)OthersBrothers-in-lawAppearancesOnlySuperfreakSeasonsGA:7Portrayed byJolene Kim Tess Adamsis the wife ofJerry Adams. Tess brought her husband, Jerry, to the hospital. He initially refused to get out of the car, but when he finally did, the doctors saw that he had warts covering most of his body. He had HPV and an immune deficiency that allowed the warts to grow out of control. His wife was upset because the warts had caused him to isolate himself in their house. He'd had two prior surgeries to remove the warts, but they'd grown back both times.

characters.name: Reed Adamson
characters.description: Reed AdamsonBiographical InformationDied2010;Seattle, WashingtonStatusDeceasedAliasTinkerbell[1]Title(s)M.D.Professional InformationProfessionSurgicalResidentWorkplaceUntil deathSeattle Grace Mercy West HospitalFormerMercy West Medical CenterAppearancesFirstInvasionLastSanctuaryCount10SeasonsGA:6Seattle Grace: On CallPortrayed byNora Zehetner Seattle Grace Mercy West HospitalFormerMercy West Medical Center Reed Adamsonwas a surgical resident atSeattle Grace Mercy West Hospitalfollowing the merger. She was murdered by grieving widowerGary Clarkbecause she refused to help him locateDerek Shepherd, as she had a patient to attend to. Reed was abrasive to those she didn't like or didn't know well, particularly the competing residents fromSeattle Grace Hospitalupon the merger. However, she lightened up as time went on.

characters.name: Carolyn Adkins
characters.description: Carolyn AdkinsBiographical InformationStatusAliveFamilial InformationMaritalHarry Adkins(husband)ChildrenWinnie Adkins†AppearancesOnlyYou Haven't Done Nothin'SeasonsGA:13Portrayed byKatherine Kirkpatrick Carolyn Adkinsis the mother ofWinnie Adkins. Carolyn was having coffee a block away from her home with her husband, Harry, when their apartment building collapsed. Their daughter, Winnie, was injured, so they drove her to the hospital. After Winnie was taken into surgery, they learned that their landlord,Micah Jacobson, knew the building wasn't sound, angering them. Harry swore his revenge, along with several of the other tenants. When they learned Winnie had died, Carolyn went to the ICU and tried to kill Micah herself by disconnecting his vent. She was restrained and then arrested.
